Company Overview

Capco is an entrepreneurial consulting business with expertise in transformation, technology, and strategy. We specialize in banking and payment; capital markets; wealth & investment management; finance, risk & compliance; and technology, serving our clients from offices in leading financial centers across US, Europe and APAC. We are expanding our business rapidly across Asia (especially Malaysia). You will work on engaging projects with some of the largest banking and insurance clients in the world, projects that will deliver significant transformation and change.

Through our collaborative and efficient approach, we help our clients successfully increase revenue, manage risk and regulatory change, reduce cost and enhance control. We specialize in banking; capital markets; wealth and investment management; finance, risk & compliance; and technology. We serve our clients from offices in leading financial centers across North America, Europe and APAC.

Role Title: Data Analyst - Finance Data (Consultant/Senior Consultant grade)

Role Overview

Join us and work with a leading global bank supporting ESG and sustainability reporting initiatives. This role focuses on finance data validation, reconciliations, data quality investigations, SQL analysis, and reporting controls across Finance and Risk stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Data Quality & Validation
  • Perform data validation checks to ensure completeness, accuracy, and consistency of finance data.
  • Analyse exception reports to identify trends, anomalies, and recurring data issues.
  • Investigate root causes of data breaks and coordinate with upstream teams to resolve them
  • Reconciliation & Adjustments
  • Perform regular reconciliations across multiple systems to ensure data alignment.
  • Identify and investigate variances, working with finance and operations teams to validate differences.
  • Execute and document data adjustments (“true-ups”) with full traceability and supporting evidence.
  • Ensure all adjustments are accurately recorded with proper classification and audit trails
  • Data Controls & Governance
  • Support reference data management by assessing change impacts and ensuring updates are properly implemented.
  • Maintain strong data governance practices, ensuring compliance with internal controls and audit requirements.
  • Prepare and maintain audit-ready documentation, including evidence packs and process records
  • Stakeholder Management
  • Collaborate with finance, risk, and data teams across regions to resolve issues and improve data quality.
  • Respond to stakeholder queries with clear explanations and supporting documentation.
  • Escalate risks, control gaps, and unresolved issues with recommended actions

Skills And Experience

  • 2–5 years’ experience in finance operations, data controls, reconciliation, or reporting, preferably in consulting or banking industry.
  • Solid understanding of reconciliation, data validation, and financial data flows.
  • Basic knowledge of accounting concepts (e.g. general ledger).
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience in root cause analysis.
  • High attention to detail with a strong control and audit mindset.
  • Comfortable working with large datasets.
  • Working knowledge of SQL (essential) for data investigation and analysis.
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to work across teams and stakeholders

Why join us?

You will join a company that supports and encourages an entrepreneurial outlook and independent thinking. Capco is not about organizational charts and layers –we operate with little hierarchy because we want all employees to feel that Capco is their firm. We warmly value diversity and inclusion and embrace our collective uniqueness –our culture is a strong, fresh, and invigorating difference from our competitors.

raw_html:
Position : Data Analyst

(BI & ETL / BI System Administrator)


Role Purpose

The Data Analyst is responsible for delivering accurate, trusted, and actionable insights by combining analytics, ETL, data warehouse, and BI platform administration. This role ensures data is correctly extracted, transformed, stored, governed, and presented, while maintaining the health, security, and usability of the BI platform and data warehouse. The Data Analyst acts as both the guardian of data integrity and the owner of the BI system and reporting data layer.


Key Responsibilities

1. Data Understanding, Extraction & Validation

  • Understand data structures and flows from core business systems (CRM, ERP, operational systems)
  • Extract data using queries, APIs, or connectors
  • Validate data accuracy, completeness, and consistency
  • Identify data gaps, anomalies, and data quality issues
  • Work with technical teams to resolve data issues at the source


2. ETL & Data Transformation

  • Design, build, and maintain ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipelines
  • Transform raw data into clean, structured datasets for reporting and analysis
  • Apply business rules, calculations, and standardisation logic
  • Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimise ETL jobs and data refresh schedules
  • Ensure data pipelines are reliable and performant


3. Data Warehouse and Cloud Data Platform Management

  • Maintain and support the reporting data warehouse or centralised data store
  • Design and manage data warehouse schemas to support analytics and reporting
  • Ensure data warehouse tables are well-structured, documented, and optimised for BI usage
  • Coordinate changes to data warehouse structures resulting from system or process changes
  • Ensure data consistency between source systems, data warehouse, and BI reports
  • Monitor, optimize, and manage cloud data platform performance and usage, including query efficiency and resource utilization


4. BI System Administration (Platform Ownership)

  • Act as the administrator and owner of the BI platform (e.g. DOMO or similar)
  • Manage users, roles, permissions, and access controls
  • Configure data connections, datasets, and refresh schedules
  • Monitor system usage, performance, and data freshness
  • Maintain BI platform configuration, documentation, and best practices


5. KPI Definition & Metric Governance

  • Work with stakeholders to define clear and consistent KPIs
  • Document metric definitions, calculation logic, and assumptions
  • Ensure KPIs are applied consistently across dashboards and reports
  • Prevent conflicting metrics and maintain a single version of truth


6. BI Reporting & Dashboard Development

  • Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports
  • Translate business questions into clear, actionable visualisations
  • Ensure dashboards are intuitive, accurate, and easy to use
  • Continuously improve reports based on user feedback


7. Business Analysis & Insights

  • Perform trend, variance, and root-cause analysis
  • Highlight risks, anomalies, and key performance drivers
  • Support management and operational reviews with data-backed insights
  • Respond to ad-hoc analytical requests


8. BI Governance, Security & Enablement

  • Enforce BI and data governance standards
  • Ensure appropriate access controls and protection of sensitive data
  • Support user onboarding and training for BI tools
  • Promote data literacy and effective use of dashboards across the organisation


9. Collaboration & Continuous Improvement

  • Work closely with business users to understand reporting needs
  • Collaborate with Application Analysts and Tech Leads during system changes
  • Validate reporting outputs during UAT and releases
  • Recommend improvements to data models, data warehouse structures, ETL logic, and BI practices


Skills & Experience

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Hands-on experience with BI platforms (dashboards, datasets, permissions)
  • Practical experience with ETL / data transformation
  • Experience working with or supporting a data warehouse or centralised reporting database
  • Solid understanding of relational data concepts (tables, joins, keys, relationships)
  • Ability to translate business questions into analytical and reporting solutions
  • SQL proficiency for data extraction, transformation, and validation
  • Experience using scripting languages (e.g. Python, PowerShell, or similar) for light automation and data preparation
  • Experience with ETL or data preparation tools
  • Familiarity with data warehouse modelling concepts (facts, dimensions, star schemas)
  • Familiarity with BI governance, dataset management, and access control
  • Experience working with CRM, ERP, or similar systems e.g. SAP, MONDAY CRM
  • Experience engaging with and managing stakeholders for both technical and non-technical audiences


Why Join Us

  • Own and shape the BI and analytics ecosystem
  • High visibility role supporting business decision-making
  • Work closely with regional and functional teams


Work Setting Requirement

  • Full time Monday to Friday based at WORQ Bandar Utama

raw_html:
Job Description

Join our world class franchise where you will be a part of a great team with a winning culture, empowered to serve our customers with high-quality set of products and services maintaining the highest standards of operational excellence.

As an Analyst within the Commercial & Investment Bank (CIB) Wholesale KYC Operations team, you ensure compliance with procedures and practices set by the CIB KYC policy team by adhering to KYC and due diligence activities. You are responsible for conducting reviews of clients' KYC documentation stored in internal repositories and publicly available information, performing necessary screenings against relevant search engines and sanction lists, and communicating any deficiencies back to the business. You will work closely with the Middle Office team to ensure KYC due diligence activities are completed on time.

Job Responsibilities

  • Liaise with the Middle Office on client onboarding/sales for required documents and sign-off
  • Review client's KYC documentation in accordance with the firm's AML/KYC policies and procedures on a timely basis
  • Validate the accuracy of data in KYC platforms and ensure the completeness of the document package
  • Complete onboarding efforts and projects to clean up data and documentation, coordinating with other areas as needed (Client Onboarding, Compliance, Sales, Client Service).
  • Act as an Subject Matter Expert (SME) and Due Diligence expert to guide the Middle Office through KYC requirements and variations of documents that may fulfill due diligence requirements
  • Conduct in-depth analysis on the ownership structure of the client using publicly available sources (not limited to company registries) or client documentation, and seek additional confirmation or approvals as required
  • Work closely with the Middle Office to obtain all necessary supporting evidence for full KYC due diligence
  • Manage personal workload and priority items, ensuring timely escalation of key risks/issues to management
  • Stay informed of any changes to processes and procedures, regulatory changes, and ensure open verbal dialogue with colleagues across the region
  • Identify and execute process improvements, providing guidance and support on key process and technology initiatives to the business

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills

  • At least 1 year of experience (Audit, Control, Risk, AML, and Research may also be areas of experience)
  • Strong working knowledge of KYC/Compliance/AML procedures, standards, and regulations
  • Knowledge of multiple client types (e.g., Corporates, SPV, Trust, FIs)
  • Experience in client Due Diligence, risk assessment, and vetting of PEP & negative news
  • Knowledge of ﬁnancial industry with in-depth expertise in various lines of business (Corporate Investment Bank and heritage lines of business)
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Strong time management, organizational, and relationship-building skills
  • Quality client focus with a strong controls mindset and customer service skills
  • Prior experience adhering to controls and compliance standards
  • Ability to grasp and learn concepts and procedures quickly
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills

  • Knowledge of KYC & Client onboarding is preferred
  • Multi language capability in either Mandarin/Japanese/Korean language (written and spoken) as the role requires interactions with the APAC stakeholders

ABOUT US

J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first-class business in a first-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

J.P. Morgan's Commercial & Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial & Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

job_description:
The group you’ll be a part ofThe Office of the CTO is where innovation takes center stage. We inspire our global technical community to take on grand challenges, understand emerging trends, identify the critical inflections, and drive our sustainability, Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG) practices that will define the next generation of semiconductors and continued impact.The impact you’ll makeAs an ESG Data Analyst at Lam, you'll analyze complex business problems and craft solutions that drive a positive global impact. You'll bridge gaps and provide technical expertise to translate business needs and data into functional visuals, elegant systems, and thoughtful analysis and recommendations. Your contributions within dynamic cross-functional teams are essential in supporting Lam’s ESG (environmental, social, and governance) journey.The ESG Data Analyst supports a variety of data management and calculation activities critical to the ongoing growth of Lam’s ESG program. raw_html:
The group you’ll be a part of

The Office of the CTO is where innovation takes center stage. We inspire our global technical community to take on grand challenges, understand emerging trends, identify the critical inflections, and drive our sustainability, Environment, Social, and Governance (ESG) practices that will define the next generation of semiconductors and continued impact.

The impact you’ll make

As an ESG Data Analyst at Lam, you'll analyze complex business problems and craft solutions that drive a positive global impact. You'll bridge gaps and provide technical expertise to translate business needs and data into functional visuals, elegant systems, and thoughtful analysis and recommendations. Your contributions within dynamic cross-functional teams are essential in supporting Lam’s ESG (environmental, social, and governance) journey.

The ESG Data Analyst supports a variety of data management and calculation activities critical to the ongoing growth of Lam’s ESG program. The role supports reporting, disclosures, and regulatory readiness; drives benchmarking, and the overall success of Lam’s sustainability, net zero, and other impact goals.

What You’ll Do

  • Support annual voluntary reporting process and data collection as well as future-state regulatory reporting
    • Collaborate with various teams to collect and validate global- and site-level data and calculate key metrics for public reporting
    • Orchestrate Lam’s annual greenhouse gas inventory
    • Complete robust data reviews, including improved data visualization and QA/QC activities
    • Follow all relevant internal procedures for data management
    • Work with vendors and internal teams to advance data rigor year-over-year
  • Deliver data-oriented analysis of Lam’s performance
    • Leverage data management platform to collect, manage, and analyze Lam’s environmental, social, and governance data
    • Develop, maintain, and evolve dashboards, especially as it relates to programs designed to help Lam meet public ESG goals
    • Engage with Logistics, Supply Chain, Global HR, EHS, Facilities, and others to deliver timely analysis and forecasting to drive programs and inform decision-making
    • Consolidate data into charts, graphs, and other visualizations as well as written analysist for storytelling, reporting, and other disclosures
    • Support development of site- and region- specific analysis
    • Build predictive models of emissions based on growth projections and business priorities
Other Responsibilities

  • Support technology transformations
    • Collaborate on the development of internal AI tools to automate responses and synthesize benchmarking information
    • Assign roles within the data management platform, set up tasks within the system
    • Build and leverage system reports and dashboards
    • Support API integrations
  • Support annual and ongoing ESG benchmarking
    • Benchmark Lam against peers and the industry across a variety of areas such as: reporting, programs, goals, materiality, and others as needed by Lam’s impact programs
  • Respond to customer inquiries about Lam’s emissions and environmental footprint
    • Review inquiries from internal partners, develop a database of approved responses, and coordinate with various team stakeholders
Who We’re Looking For

  • Bachelor’s degree with 8+ years of experience; or Master’s degree with 6+ years’ experience; or equivalent experience.
  • 6+ years of experience in environmental engineering, natural resource management, sustainability, data science, or related field.
  • Proficiency with data models, database design and management, segmentation, modeling
  • Strong data management, quality assurance, analysis, and visualization skills
  • Excellent communication skills with the ability to distill complex information and ideas to meet the needs of a wide range of stakeholder audiences
  • Ability to work collaboratively across various internal teams with different data needs, interests, and capabilities
  • Tools:
    • Power BI, Tableau, Excel SQL, Python, other data calculation and visualization tools
    • ESG data management platforms (e.g. Metrio, Watershed, Workiva, etc.)
Preferred Qualifications

  • The following knowledge and skills are desirable but not necessary:
    • Project management experience
    • Detail-oriented with exceptional organizational skills
    • Experience with SQL and databases
    • Experience with GHG Protocol and science-based targets
    • Familiarity with global reporting standards and frameworks is desirable. This includes but is not limited to: GRI, SASB, TCFD, ISSB, California, EU, Malaysia, etc.
    • Experience with or interest in automation integration, API/data lake architecture, agentic AI
    • Experience with ESG disclosure and rater frameworks (CDP, CSA, MSCI, ISS, etc.)
    • Experience with freight emissions tools (EcoTransIT, Pedge.io, Gryn, etc.)
Our commitment

We believe it is important for every person to feel valued, included, and empowered to achieve their full potential. By bringing unique individuals and viewpoints together, we achieve extraordinary results.

Lam Research ("Lam" or the "Company") is an equal opportunity employer. Lam is committed to and reaffirms support of equal opportunity in employment and non-discrimination in employment policies, practices and procedures on the basis of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions), gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, sexual orientation, or military and veteran status or any other category protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws. It is the Company's intention to comply with all applicable laws and regulations. Company policy prohibits unlawful discrimination against applicants or employees.

Lam offers a variety of work location models based on the needs of each role. Our hybrid roles combine the benefits of on-site collaboration with colleagues and the flexibility to work remotely and fall into two categories – On-site Flex and Virtual Flex. ‘On-site Flex’ you’ll work 3+ days per week on-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, with the opportunity to work remotely for the balance of the week. ‘Virtual Flex’ you’ll work 1-2 days per week on-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, and remotely the rest of the time.

raw_html:
Key Responsibilities

  • Data Analysis & Business Insights
  • Collect, clean, and analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and business opportunities.
  • Provide actionable insights to support retail and supply chain decision-making.
  • Dashboarding & Analytics
  • Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and analytical tools to monitor business performance, inventory health, and key operational metrics.
  • What-if Simulation
  • Develop simulation models to evaluate business scenarios and assess the impact of changes in pricing, assortment, inventory, display, and operational strategies.
  • Allocation & Inventory Optimization
  • Design and implement optimization solutions for inventory allocation and replenishment across stores while balancing demand, stock availability, and operational constraints.
  • Forecasting & Model Evaluation (Added Advantage)
  • Build and maintain forecasting models.
  • Evaluate model performance using appropriate metrics.
  • Continuously improve prediction accuracy.
  • Process Improvement & Automation (Added Advantage)
  • Identify process gaps and inefficiencies.
  • Propose data-driven solutions.
  • Develop automation tools to improve operational efficiency and business productivity.

Requirements

  • A background in analytics, business, economics, engineering, mathematics, or related fields.
  • 1–2 years of experience in retail, supply chain, FMCG, procurement, or related industries is preferred.
  • Proficiency in data analysis tools (Excel, SQL, BI tools, Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn)).
  • Strong ability to communicate analytical findings to stakeholders.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Able to think from a business perspective first.

MR D.I.Y. is a home-grown enterprise with over 1,300 stores across Malaysia and Brunei. The home improvement retailer has dedicated to make a positive difference in the lives of its valued customers by offering convenience at all its stores nationwide and online at mrdiy.com.my

All MR. D.I.Y. stores are managed directly and the retailer often works in collaboration with owners of shopfront properties or owners of malls. MR. D.I.Y. stores offer a wide selection of SKUs across five major categories, namely hardware; household and furnishing; electrical; stationery and sports equipment products; and others (comprising toys, car accessories, jewellery, cosmetics, food and beverage items, as well as health and personal care).

The Company strives to put its customers first by operating an innovative business that is flexible when it comes to providing a wide variety of products, good quality and value-for-money, holding true to the Company’s motto of “Always Low Prices”.

Job Responsibilities:

  • Conduct data analysis, spotting data trends, performing gap analysis, generating reports, and recommending improvements to current business metric collection process
  • Produce and deliver tracking and reporting for Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
  • Design key dashboards and scorecards for Senior Leadership and Business Unit Team Leaders, Training Team, Quality Analysts, etc.
  • Perform ad hoc data analysis to support business initiatives
  • Address reporting and data related questions from the team
  • Seek opportunities to drive innovation and process improvement through data driven decision making
  • Own weekly/monthly/quarterly operating rhythms (pipeline reviews, forecast calls, QBRs, business reviews).
  • Create agendas, pre-reads, action tracking, and follow-through to ensure decisions turn into execution.
  • Partner with Sales Leadership to define success metrics and operational expectations.
  • Identify operational bottlenecks (handoffs, approvals, deal desk dependencies) and propose fixes.
  • Ensure change management when new processes, policies, or tooling are introduced.


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree OR High School Diploma plus a minimum of 2 years of relevant work experience
  • Minimum 3 years of experience in a reporting or analytics environment, with proven ability to deliver high quality and accurate reports within demanding timescales
  • Significant experience creating reports and dashboards within Google Sheets, Excel, Salesforce, and Tableau
  • Demonstrated ability to recognize reporting process gaps and recommend improvements
  • Demonstrated ability to clearly communicate complex quantitative analysis into actionable insights
  • Good operational rigor: ability to run cadences, manage stakeholders, and enforce process standards.
  • Excellent program management skills: timelines, dependencies, documentation, follow-ups.
  • This role involves work where accurate color recognition is required (e.g., reading color-coded data, creating data visualization, presentation deck etc)

raw_html:
Job Summary

As a team member in International Project Management Office (IPMO) at MR DIY, you will play a key role in supporting the management of our international operations, particularly in Spain, Turkey, and other emerging markets. This position is integral to maintaining operational alignment, facilitating strategic initiatives, and addressing regional challenges to drive MR DIY’s international growth and success. If you thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and have a passion for working end-to-end from strategy into operations, this opportunity is for you.

Key Responsibilities

  • Performance Monitoring: Oversee and track the business performance of MR DIY’s international operations, promptly escalating key issues to senior management.
  • Coordination: Serve as the main point of contact between local management teams and regional Heads of Departments (HODs)/Managers.
  • Problem Solving: Work to resolve challenges raised by country managers by providing solutions, consulting with regional HODs/Managers, or escalating issues to senior management when necessary.
  • Research and Analysis: Conduct research and analysis to aid in strategic decision-making.
  • Project Management: Follow up with relevant parties to ensure the successful execution and monitoring of strategic initiatives and pilot projects.
  • International Expansion: Support activities necessary for the development and expansion of MR DIY’s businesses in new markets
  • Ad-Hoc Support: Provide additional support to senior management, which may include: incorporating companies in new countries, with attention to local shareholding and tax regulations. Reviewing existing corporate structures to align with investment objectives. Liaising with external advisors and service providers as required

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree or Postgraduate Diploma in Accounting, Finance, Data Analytics or any other related field.
  • Minimum of 0-2 years of relevant experience.
  • Proficient in MS Office; strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities.
  • Soft Skills: Excellent organizational, verbal, and written communication skills in both English and Mandarin.
  • Character Traits: Highly adaptable, can-do attitude, open minded, resourceful and proactive with strong initiative to meet business demands

At MR DIY International, we're more than a global home improvement brand, we're a catalyst for ambitious talent ready to grow beyond borders.

With over 5,000+ stores across 15 countries globally, we offer unmatched international exposure to those looking to build a meaningful, global career.

From retail operations and merchandising to strategy, tech, and supply chain. Your work here shapes how millions of customers shop every day.

Our Culture Is Built On GIVE

Growth: Rapid development opportunities at every stage of your journey.

International Exposure: Cross-border collaborations and secondments.

Value Creation: Real ownership, real impact.

Empowerment: A culture that trusts you to lead and innovate.

If you're driven, adaptable, and eager to be part of a high-impact team, MR DIY International is where you build, lead, and thrive.

This role is urgent and designed for an early-career professional eager to develop strong business analysis fundamentals while contributing to high-visibility, multinational projects.The role acts as a key link between business users and IT teams, supporting requirement gathering, analysis, documentation, testing, and change activities under the guidance of senior team members. It also serves as a quality gate to ensure that vendor-delivered solutions are practical, usable, and aligned with business expectations across data, automation, and AI initiatives.Hiring Manager: Operations• Assist in collecting, clarifying, and documenting business requirements through interviews, workshops, and working sessions• Help translate business needs into clear functional and data requirements• Prepare presentations, summaries, and analysis to support decision-making2) Cross-Functional & Global Collaboration• Support initiatives spanning multiple functions and markets in a dashboards, automation flows) to ensure they are usable and aligned with business needs• Reviewing data logic and providing feedback or challenge where needed4) Delivery & Quality Support• Create and maintain documentation such as business requirements, user stories, process flows, and test scenarios• Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) planning, execution, defect tracking, and follow-up• Assist with solution walkthroughs and ensure deliverables meet documented requirements• Review and validate vendor deliverables (e.g. reports, dashboards, automation flows, AI use cases)• Ensure outputs are accurate, usable, and aligned with business requirements• Identify gaps, inconsistencies, and improvement opportunities, and work closely with vendors to refine solutions5) Change & Adoption• Support best practices• Power BI developmentMust-Have Requirements• Ability to read and speak Chinese fluently due to business market need.• Bachelor’s degree in business, Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field• 3–5 years of experience in Business Analysis, IT, Data, or Digital projects (or strong relevant internship/project exposure)• Strong interest in data, analytics, automation, and AI in a business contextNice-to-Have Requirements• Exposure to FMCG, consumer goods, or multinational environments• Familiarity with Agile ways of working (Scrum, Kanban, About Company

Suntory Beverage & Food Health Enrichment (SBFHE) is a business unit under Suntory Beverage & Food Asia (SBF Asia), dedicated to delivering innovative health and wellness solutions across Asia. Our portfolio includes trusted brands and products that support healthier lifestyles, serving markets such as Thailand, Singapore, Malaysia, Hong Kong, and Taiwan. Guided by Suntory’s philosophy of “Growing for Good”, we strive to enrich lives by creating products that are natural, safe, and beneficial, bringing happiness and well-being to our consumers every day.


Role Purpose

The IT Business Analyst will support cross-functional digital initiatives across data, analytics, automation, and AI within a global FMCG environment. This role is urgent and designed for an early-career professional eager to develop strong business analysis fundamentals while contributing to high-visibility, multinational projects.

The role acts as a key link between business users and IT teams, supporting requirement gathering, analysis, documentation, testing, and change activities under the guidance of senior team members. It also serves as a quality gate to ensure that vendor-delivered solutions are practical, usable, and aligned with business expectations across data, automation, and AI initiatives.


Hiring Manager: Data, Automation & AI Lead


Compensation & Benefits:

  • 13-month salary upon completion of 12 months service (1-year contract role)
  • 12 days Annual Leave
  • Medical leave, sick leave, hospitalization leave
  • Medical insurance coverage
  • Office parking allowance


Key Responsibilities

1) Business & Stakeholder Support

• Support engagement with business stakeholders across Commercial, Supply Chain, Finance, Marketing, and Operations

• Assist in collecting, clarifying, and documenting business requirements through interviews, workshops, and working sessions

• Help translate business needs into clear functional and data requirements

• Prepare presentations, summaries, and analysis to support decision-making


2) Cross-Functional & Global Collaboration

• Support initiatives spanning multiple functions and markets in a multinational FMCG environment

• Coordinate with regional and global IT teams, vendors, and delivery partners

• Help ensure alignment between global standards and local business needs


3) Data, Analytics, Automation & AI Initiatives

• Power BI Development

• Support project work related to data platforms, BI & reporting, process automation (RPA), and AI use cases

• Assist with basic data analysis, KPI tracking, and validation of reports and dashboards

• Participate in requirement definition to ensure solutions are practical, scalable, and business-focused

• Validating vendor outputs (reports, dashboards, automation flows) to ensure they are usable and aligned with business needs

• Reviewing data logic and providing feedback or challenge where needed


4) Delivery & Quality Support

• Create and maintain documentation such as business requirements, user stories, process flows, and test scenarios

• Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) planning, execution, defect tracking, and follow-up

• Assist with solution walkthroughs and ensure deliverables meet documented requirements

• Review and validate vendor deliverables (e.g. reports, dashboards, automation flows, AI use cases)

• Ensure outputs are accurate, usable, and aligned with business requirements

• Identify gaps, inconsistencies, and improvement opportunities, and work closely with vendors to refine solutions


5) Change & Adoption

• Support change management activities including user communications, training materials, and adoption tracking

• Help collect feedback post-implementation and identify improvement opportunities


Required Qualifications & Experience

Skills & Competencies

• Solid analytical and problem-solving skills

• Basic understanding of business processes and IT systems

• Ability to document requirements clearly and structurally

• Good communication skills and confidence working with diverse stakeholders

• Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment

• Willingness to learn and apply business analysis best practices

• Power BI development


Must-Have Requirements

• Ability to read and speak Chinese fluently due to business market need.

• Bachelor’s degree in business, Information Systems, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field

• 3–5 years of experience in Business Analysis, IT, Data, or Digital projects (or strong relevant internship/project exposure)

• Strong interest in data, analytics, automation, and AI in a business context


Nice-to-Have Requirements

• Exposure to FMCG, consumer goods, or multinational environments

• Familiarity with Agile ways of working (Scrum, Kanban, hybrid delivery)

• Experience with data or reporting tools (e.g. Power BI, Excel, SQL, or similar)

• Experience or exposure to automation or analytics-related projects

Company: 6255 UOB Innovation Hub 2 Sdn Bhd

About UOB

United Overseas Bank Limited (UOB) is a leading bank in Asia with a global network of more than 500 branches and offices in 19 countries and territories in Asia Pacific, Europe and North America. In Asia, we operate through our head office in Singapore and banking subsidiaries in China, Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand, as well as branches and offices. Our history spans more than 80 years. Over this time, we have been guided by our values – Honorable, Enterprising, United and Committed. This means we always strive to do what is right, build for the future, work as one team and pursue long-term success. It is how we work, consistently, be it towards the company, our colleagues or our customers.

Job Description

About The Department

UOB Innovation Hub 2 (InnoHub2) is a UOB-wholly owned subsidiary and a Centre of Excellence based in Malaysia, providing Group Business Services (GBS) to support the UOB Group. Started in 2021 with technology application design, development and support, InnoHub2 is expanding beyond technology services to deliver other business services to support the Bank’s growth ambition. We are looking for talented and motivated individuals to be part of the pioneer team, spearheading the development and delivery of new services.

As part of the InnoHub2 team, you will have the opportunity to work on Group initiatives and gain regional business exposure. We are dynamic, passionate, and purposeful about delivering trusted financial solutions that enable business growth. An exciting career progression with varied opportunities awaits you at IH2. Come grow your career with us.

Job Responsibilities

The Senior Data Analyst is responsible for gathering and analyzing data to guide management’s decision making, identifying data trends, and exploring and highlighting potential improvements. Based on stakeholder needs, this technical role identifies requirements, and designs and implements data related systems which include exploring multiple data sources, building data pipelines, automating processes, analyzing, and breaking down the results and communicating the results in various forms such as metrics / reports / KPIs /dashboards and similar, and providing refinement suggestions based on findings from various data sources.

  • Provide technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing, and perform data profiling and gap analysis, source to target mappings, data issue capturing, resolution and escalation, including identifying scope creep, improving data quality and others
  • Drive complex data analysis to identify, analyze, and predict trends or patterns associated with potential performance/audit/compliance risks, and lead and implement automation in data compilation and report/KPI generation for analysis use in management meetings, including performance and key deliverables of projects.
  • Standardize the reports, KPIs and performance measurement metrics across Programs and Projects
  • Create and maintain key data sets and ways of collecting data, use different tools and technologies such as SQL, R, Python, eazyBI, Power BI, Jira, Confluence, and like to conduct timely ad-hoc and scheduled data/report/KPI preparation requests, perform data, transactional and trend analysis from multiple sources, and build visualization dashboard/generate reports for various stakeholders
  • Perform end to end analytic solutions: from understanding business requirements, data discovery and extraction to model development and evaluation, and collaborate and work closely with stakeholders to identify opportunities for leveraging data to drive business outcomes. Also execute exploratory data analysis to answer business questions and inform business decisions, identify data sources and provisioning of data into enterprise data platforms
  • Conduct training to end-users on new reports/KPIs and dashboards, partner with and support users in other internal teams to develop and deliver data science solutions to further value-add in performance/audit reviews.
  • Ensure compliance with data governance and best practices and standards set forth by bank policies and requirements.
  • Prepare and maintain documentation for data collection, management, and reporting
  • Work with Agile/Tribe Coaches to guide and improve Ways of Working for agile teams by revamping data collection and leveraging data, and generate weekly project reports for the teams to monitor their progress and highlight any inconsistencies, impediments, areas of improvement and similar

Job Requirements

  • Minimum bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional qualifications in Computer Science, Business Information Systems, Mathematics, Statistics, or similar quantitative discipline, with at least 4-6 years of total work experience in banking/finance industry, preferably with business / functional knowledge
  • Basic knowledge in banking operations is required. Having experience as a data analyst or in a related role/field in banking/finance industry is highly desirable
  • Excellent command of Excel, and fluent in one or more programming languages, such as React JS (must have), Python, SQL, SAS, and R (good to have)
  • At least 3 years of hand-on experience in MS SQL or Jira or Confluence or Qlik Sense or Power BI or eazyBI and relevant data/report-related plugins for these systems
  • Experience in gathering and documentation of business and technical requirements for data collection, analysis, maintenance, and reporting purposes
  • Proficient in designing, developing, modeling, and documenting data-driven solutions – including capturing / creating / utilizing data architectures, metadata systems, data dictionaries, databases, applications, pipelines, BI Tools, Big Data Platforms, analytical /query, and visualization tools
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy, and keen to deep dive into data to find answers to yet unknown questions and have a natural desire to go beneath the surface of a problem
  • Exceptional data visualization and communication skills to present findings and insights to senior management and key stakeholders, including non-technical business users.
  • Excellent written / oral communication skills to effectively interact with different stakeholders and external vendors, able to work independently under pressure
  • Ability to work effectively in a team-fostered, multi-tasking environment, and a proactive team player who is curious about data and possesses a strong drive to create and lead initiatives

Additional Requirements

Develop, Engage, Execute, Strategise

Be a Part of the UOB Family

UOB is an equal opportunity employer. UOB does not discriminate on the basis of a candidate's age, race, gender, color, religion, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, or other non-merit factors. All employment decisions at UOB are based on business needs, job requirements and qualifications. If you require any assistance or accommodations to be made for the recruitment process, please inform us when you submit your online application.

Apply now and make a Difference

About Hytech

Hytech is a leading management consulting firm headquartered in Australia and Singapore, specialising in digital transformation for fintech and financial services organisations. We deliver end-to-end consulting services and provide robust middle- and back-office solutions that enable our clients to optimise operations, enhance efficiency, and stay ahead in a fast-evolving digital landscape. Our client portfolio includes top global trading platforms and leading crypto exchanges.


With more than 2,000 professionals worldwide, Hytech has a strong and growing international presence, with offices across Australia, Singapore, Malaysia, Taiwan, the Philippines, Thailand, Morocco, Cyprus, Dubai, and beyond.


Role Overview:

We are looking for a detail-oriented and curious Data Analyst to support regulatory, compliance, and finance data reporting.

This role is ideal for candidates who are eager to develop financial regulations, audit data structures, and business workflows while building strong technical data skills.

You will work closely with senior data leads, compliance, finance, and risk teams to ensure accurate and traceable regulatory data delivery across multiple jurisdictions.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Support regulatory and financial reporting data preparation and validation
  • Assist in building and maintaining data pipelines for compliance and audit reporting
  • Extract and analyze data from Data Lake / Databricks / SQL environments
  • Ensure accuracy and consistency of regulatory and finance data outputs
  • Assist in monitoring regulatory reporting requirements and internal data definitions
  • Work closely with Compliance, Finance, Risk, and Data teams on daily data requests
  • Document data logic, definitions, and reporting processes for audit traceability
  • Continuously improve data automation and reporting efficiency


Qualifications:

  • Fluent in English and Mandarin (written and spoken)
  • 1–3 years experience in data analysis, finance, audit, or compliance-related roles
  • Strong proficiency in SQL & Python
  • Basic understanding of financial services / trading / fintech industry preferred
  • Detail-oriented with strong data validation and problem-solving skills
  • Willingness to learn regulatory, audit, and financial reporting frameworks


Preferred Qualifications

  • Internship or experience in Big4 / audit / financial services
  • Exposure to Python, Databricks, or data lake environments
  • Familiarity with trading data (CFD, equities, derivatives) is a plus
  • Experience handling large datasets and reconciliation tasks
  • Strong documentation and communication skills

Who We AreGXBank is the FIRST digital bank in Malaysia approved by Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM). Backed by Grab, our mission is simple yet powerful: to leverage cutting-edge technology to serve the unserved and underserved—the everyday Malaysians and MSMEs who need it most.We are a team driven by passion, innovation, and a shared purpose to redefine the financial landscape. If you're tired of legacy systems and want to build something that truly matters, you belong here.The RoleAs our Lead Specialist for Regional Business Intelligence (BI), you will be the strategic voice behind our product innovation, turning complex data into actionable experiments that shape the bank's future. This isn't just an analytics role; it’s an opportunity to own the data lifecycle, pioneer Generative AI initiatives, and directly influence how we build and scale products at GXBank.What You’ll Be DoingPartner with product, design, and engineering teams to derive insights, design experiments, and drive key product decisions.Support strategic plans with data insights, performing deep-dives into user behavior, segmented views and product efficacy.Provide tailored analysis for specific products and strategies, define critical business and product metrics, and make regular recommendations for continuous improvements.Design, measure, and recommend A/B tests and multivariate experiments, including layout optimisation, UI/UX efficacy, algorithm effectiveness, and API performance.Analyse clickstream and transactional data to uncover insights on user behaviour and drive product metrics. You will manage instrumentation for all feature releases within assigned tech families within the bank.Develop self-serve solutions for stakeholders that are scalable and automated to handle a product environment. Drive plans involving Large Language Models or Generative Artificial Intelligence.Who We Are Looking ForBachelor's Degree in Statistics, Analytics, Economics, Mathematics, Engineering, or related fields.Minimum 5 years of experience in Analytics, Business Intelligence, or Data Science, preferably in a Bank or Tech (FinTech or E-Commerce) company.Proficient in writing advanced SQL queries, Python and querying large relational databases.Skilled in data visualisation platforms like Tableau or HTML to present findings.Strong experience in translating data insights into relevant recommendations for non-technical and senior team members.Experience in designing, running, and analysing A/B tests and product experiments, with knowledge of hypothesis testing and the basic principles of Design of Experiments (DoE).

raw_html:
Who We Are

GXBank is the FIRST digital bank in Malaysia approved by Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM). Backed by Grab, our mission is simple yet powerful: to leverage cutting-edge technology to serve the unserved and underserved—the everyday Malaysians and MSMEs who need it most.

We are a team driven by passion, innovation, and a shared purpose to redefine the financial landscape. If you're tired of legacy systems and want to build something that truly matters, you belong here.

The Role

As our Lead Specialist for Regional Business Intelligence (BI), you will be the strategic voice behind our product innovation, turning complex data into actionable experiments that shape the bank's future. This isn't just an analytics role; it’s an opportunity to own the data lifecycle, pioneer Generative AI initiatives, and directly influence how we build and scale products at GXBank.

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Partner with product, design, and engineering teams to derive insights, design experiments, and drive key product decisions.
  • Support strategic plans with data insights, performing deep-dives into user behavior, segmented views and product efficacy.
  • Provide tailored analysis for specific products and strategies, define critical business and product metrics, and make regular recommendations for continuous improvements.
  • Design, measure, and recommend A/B tests and multivariate experiments, including layout optimisation, UI/UX efficacy, algorithm effectiveness, and API performance.
  • Analyse clickstream and transactional data to uncover insights on user behaviour and drive product metrics. You will manage instrumentation for all feature releases within assigned tech families within the bank.
  • Develop self-serve solutions for stakeholders that are scalable and automated to handle a product environment.
  • Drive plans involving Large Language Models or Generative Artificial Intelligence.

Who We Are Looking For

  • Bachelor's Degree in Statistics, Analytics, Economics, Mathematics, Engineering, or related fields.
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in Analytics, Business Intelligence, or Data Science, preferably in a Bank or Tech (FinTech or E-Commerce) company.
  • Proficient in writing advanced SQL queries, Python and querying large relational databases.
  • Skilled in data visualisation platforms like Tableau or HTML to present findings.
  • Strong experience in translating data insights into relevant recommendations for non-technical and senior team members.
  • Experience in designing, running, and analysing A/B tests and product experiments, with knowledge of hypothesis testing and the basic principles of Design of Experiments (DoE).

raw_html:
About the Role

We are looking for passionate, driven, and technically sound Data Engineers at all career stages-from ambitious Juniors to visionary Leads-to join our growing data engineering team. If you thrive in a collaborative environment, love building robust data pipelines, and are excited about working onsite with a dynamic team, we want to hear from you.

As a Data Engineer, you will design, build, and optimize our data ecosystem to handle large-scale, real-time data streaming and processing.

Key Responsibilities

  • Pipeline Development: Design, develop, and maintain scalable, robust batch and real-time data pipelines.

  • Streaming & Processing: Leverage Kafka and Flink to handle high-throughput, low-latency streaming data.

  • Data Architecture: Optimize data storage, ingestion, and transformation processes to ensure high availability and performance.

  • Collaboration: Work closely with Data Scientists, Analysts, and Product teams to deliver clean, reliable data products.

  • Leadership (For Senior/Lead roles): Mentor junior engineers, architect complex data systems, and drive technical best practices across the team.

What We Are Looking For



Core Technical Stack (Required):

  • Streaming Tools: Strong hands-on experience with Apache Kafka and/or Apache Flink is a must.

  • Programming: Proficiency in Python, Java, or Scala.

  • Databases & Warehousing: Experience with SQL/NoSQL databases and modern cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift).

  • Big Data Ecosystem: Familiarity with Spark, Hadoop, or Hive is highly advantageous.

Experience Levels & Expectations:

  • Junior: 1-3 years of experience. Eager to learn, strong foundational coding, and basic exposure to data pipelines.

  • Mid-Level: 3-5 years of experience. Ability to own pipeline development independently and troubleshoot streaming issues.

  • Senior: 5-8 years of experience. Deep understanding of system design, performance tuning in Kafka/Flink, and architectural patterns.

  • Lead: 8+ years of experience. Proven track record of leading engineering teams, defining data strategy, and architecting enterprise-grade data platforms.

What's in It for You?

  • Competitive Compensation: Earn up to MYR 20,000 per month, tailored strictly to your years of experience, technical depth, and interview performance.

  • Career Growth: Clear progression pathways from Junior all the way to Lead roles.

  • Collaborative Culture: A vibrant, onsite working environment where you can brainstorm on whiteboards and innovate alongside peers daily.

  • Comprehensive Benefits: Health insurance, performance bonuses, and continuous learning allowances.

location: Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
job_description:
Role: Data Governance and Quality AnalystLocation: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia Job type: Full time, permanent – hybridThis position offers a hybrid work model, allowing flexibility between working from home and our office. Typically, employees are expected to work 2 days in the office per week.Why QS?At QS, we believe that work should empower you. That’s why we foster a flexible working environment that encourages every employee to own their career whilst flourishing personally and professionally.Our commitment to people development is recognised globally: QS is a Gold-accredited Investors in People organisation, placing us among the top 28% of workplaces worldwide. Our company values underpin everything we do – we collaborate, respect and support each other.It’s our mission to empower motivated people around the world to fulfil their potential through higher education, ensuring that everyone has access to opportunities that change lives.Our diversity makes us stronger. By sharing our experiences, we learn from one another and achieve more together, driving progress across the sector.At QS, you’ll be responsible for implementing real change in the international higher education landscape. You’ll take on meaningful challenges that see a positive impact across the business and the wider sector.We’re confident you’ll feel right at home here. QS was named as one of Newsweek’s Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces® in the UK (October 2023), recognising the respect, trust and appreciation that drive our culture every day.As a Data Governance and Quality Analyst, this is what you’ll be doing:The Data Governance & Quality Analyst will be responsible for establishing, operating, and continuously improving governance and quality standards across our centralised data platforms, including the data warehouse, data lake, and related analytics environments. This role ensures that data entering and residing within these platforms is accurate, validated, trustworthy, documented, and aligned to organisational needs.Unlike traditional compliance‑focused roles, this position champions quality, structure, lifecycle management, and controlled development practices. The Analyst will define data quality rules, validation frameworks, access role definitions, and data standards, ensuring our data platforms become stable, scalable, and high‑quality assets.A key focus of the role will be addressing existing issues in our datasets — profiling, assessing, and remediating legacy data — and determining what should be retained, archived, or rebuilt. The role will partner closely with data engineering, data acquisition, and compliance, ensuring governance supports sustainable data engineering practices rather than restricting them.Role responsibilities:Data Quality, Validation & VerificationDesign, implement, and maintain data quality frameworks and validation rules for all data entering centralised data platforms.Develop quality checks, verification workflows, and monitoring dashboards for ongoing data accuracy and completeness.Partner with data engineers to embed automated controls within ingestion and transformation pipelines.Conduct regular data profiling and root‑cause analysis to identify and resolve quality issues.Document and maintain a catalogue of quality rules, validation logic, and known data risks.Data Governance Standards & ControlsEstablish and enforce standards for naming conventions, dataset structures, metadata requirements, and lineage documentation.Review new datasets for structural, governance, and quality compliance before onboarding into centralised data platforms.Define best‑practice guidance for engineers on dataset creation, transformation, and management.Access Roles, raw_html:
Role: Data Governance and Quality Analyst

Location: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

Job type: Full time, permanent – hybrid


This position offers a hybrid work model, allowing flexibility between working from home and our office. Typically, employees are expected to work 2 days in the office per week.


Why QS?


At QS, we believe that work should empower you. That’s why we foster a flexible working environment that encourages every employee to own their career whilst flourishing personally and professionally.


Our commitment to people development is recognised globally: QS is a Gold-accredited Investors in People organisation, placing us among the top 28% of workplaces worldwide. Our company values underpin everything we do – we collaborate, respect and support each other.


It’s our mission to empower motivated people around the world to fulfil their potential through higher education, ensuring that everyone has access to opportunities that change lives.


Our diversity makes us stronger. By sharing our experiences, we learn from one another and achieve more together, driving progress across the sector.


At QS, you’ll be responsible for implementing real change in the international higher education landscape. You’ll take on meaningful challenges that see a positive impact across the business and the wider sector.


We’re confident you’ll feel right at home here. QS was named as one of Newsweek’s Top 100 Most Loved Workplaces® in the UK (October 2023), recognising the respect, trust and appreciation that drive our culture every day.


As a Data Governance and Quality Analyst, this is what you’ll be doing:


The Data Governance & Quality Analyst will be responsible for establishing, operating, and continuously improving governance and quality standards across our centralised data platforms, including the data warehouse, data lake, and related analytics environments. This role ensures that data entering and residing within these platforms is accurate, validated, trustworthy, documented, and aligned to organisational needs.


Unlike traditional compliance‑focused roles, this position champions quality, structure, lifecycle management, and controlled development practices. The Analyst will define data quality rules, validation frameworks, access role definitions, and data standards, ensuring our data platforms become stable, scalable, and high‑quality assets.


A key focus of the role will be addressing existing issues in our datasets — profiling, assessing, and remediating legacy data — and determining what should be retained, archived, or rebuilt. The role will partner closely with data engineering, data acquisition, and compliance, ensuring governance supports sustainable data engineering practices rather than restricting them.


Role responsibilities:


Data Quality, Validation & Verification

  • Design, implement, and maintain data quality frameworks and validation rules for all data entering centralised data platforms.
  • Develop quality checks, verification workflows, and monitoring dashboards for ongoing data accuracy and completeness.
  • Partner with data engineers to embed automated controls within ingestion and transformation pipelines.
  • Conduct regular data profiling and root‑cause analysis to identify and resolve quality issues.
  • Document and maintain a catalogue of quality rules, validation logic, and known data risks.


Data Governance Standards & Controls

  • Establish and enforce standards for naming conventions, dataset structures, metadata requirements, and lineage documentation.
  • Review new datasets for structural, governance, and quality compliance before onboarding into centralised data platforms.
  • Define best‑practice guidance for engineers on dataset creation, transformation, and management.


Access Roles, Permissions & Controls

  • Define data access roles to ensure a principle‑based, controlled access model.
  • Advise central IT on how these roles should be structured and implemented (while IT retains operational control of user provisioning).
  • Develop role‑based access principles aligned to security, data sensitivity, and governance needs.
  • Liaise with the Compliance Officer to ensure alignment with regulatory obligations without creating operational bottlenecks.


Legacy Data Assessment & Remediation

  • Audit and catalogue existing datasets across centralised data platforms.
  • Evaluate datasets for quality, usage, consistency, risk, and strategic value.
  • Recommend and lead remediation actions: retain, repair, consolidate, archive, or deprecate.
  • Support engineering teams to safely rebuild or retire legacy structures.


Governance Roadmap & Continuous Improvement

  • Develop and maintain a roadmap for maturing data governance and quality capabilities across the organisation.
  • Track governance adoption and identify opportunities to streamline processes.
  • Provide training and communication to promote data quality awareness and shared accountability.
  • Ensure governance supports analytics, reporting, and data science teams through clarity, consistency, and improved data trust.


Stakeholder Engagement

  • Act as the primary point of contact for governance and quality matters across engineering, IT, analytics, and compliance.
  • Facilitate discussions related to data quality issues, source system changes, and process improvements.
  • Communicate governance updates and data quality findings to internal stakeholders.



Key skills and experience:

  • Experience in data governance, data quality, data management, data engineering, or similar roles.
  • Strong understanding of data quality frameworks, validation processes, and root‑cause analysis.
  • Experience working with data lake or cloud‑based data platforms.
  • Proficiency with SQL and data profiling techniques; familiarity with Python or ETL/ELT tooling is an excellent advantage.
  • Experience working collaboratively with data engineering, IT, and compliance functions.
  • Strong documentation skills, with the ability to articulate governance and quality standards.
  • Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail.
  • Excellent communication skills for both technical and non‑technical audiences.
  • Ability to balance governance rigour with practical, outcome‑oriented decision‑making.


So, who are we and what do we do?


QS is the world leader in higher education services, analytics, insights and intelligence. From consultancy to student mobility, academic partnerships to branding solutions, our services power both institutional and individual success.


We’re behind the world’s most widely read university rankings. With an 85% share of all Google searches for university rankings, the QS World University Rankings® reach hundreds of millions, shaping decisions and guiding futures.


Since launching in 1990, our impact and influence have only grown. Today, we work with more than 2,000 of the world’s leading higher education institutions, over 12,000 employers, and governments seeking change and socioeconomic development through higher education – while helping more than 75,000 students enrol each year.


Join QS and you’ll join an 800-strong community of problem-solvers, creators, collaborators and change-makers based in 40+ countries and 11

international offices, including Australia, Malaysia, India, Romania, Singapore, France, Germany, the USA and our headquarters in London.


With every talented new hire, business acquisition and bold initiative, we’re strengthening our reach and delivering even greater value to institutions and learners worldwide. Are you ready to shape the future of higher education?


We take investing in our people very seriously.


As standard you will have:


  • Competitive base salary
  • Access to an annual bonus scheme (for qualifying roles only)
  • 20 days annual leave, plus public holidays
  • Generous maternity and paternity leave
  • Access to an Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and MiCare health
  • A vibrant social environment and multicultural and multinational culture


But that’s not all. Outside of these standard benefits we also offer resources to allow professional growth and wellness initiatives to nurture a healthy mindset:


  • Free subscription to the Calm App – the #1 app for sleep, meditation, and relaxation
  • A focus on welfare which is led by our global wellness team, with mental health first aiders globally
  • Access to a variety of diversity and inclusion initiatives and groups
  • Strong recognition and reward programs – including a peer-to-peer recognition platform, quarterly and annual QS Applaud Awards, Connect with your Career annual PD event
  • Support for volunteering and study leave
  • Free subscription to LinkedIn learning – with over 5000 courses and programmes at your fingertips
  • Options to join our outstanding global Mentorship programme
  • Brand new breakout and relaxing spaces in office


Like what you’ve heard? Great, apply now!


As a candidate, we know the application and interview process can be daunting and so it’s important that you have a great experience with us. Our dedicated Talent Team will work hard to ensure you are fully informed at all stages and you are really excited by this opportunity to do meaningful work in the education space.


Equal opportunities


QS Quacquarelli Symonds is proud to be a fair and equal organisation where everyone has the same opportunity to achieve their full potential, irrespective of their background or personal attributes. We celebrate our diversity and believe through sharing our experiences we can learn from one another, be stronger together, and enable our business to thrive.

  • What-if Simulation

    • Assess impact of pricing, display, and stock changes.
  • Allocation Optimization
    • Design optimization models for inventory allocation across stores.
    • Balance constraints such as stock availability, demand, and capacity.
  • Dashboarding and Analytics
    • Create and maintain analytics that is impactful and able to drive business on various platform eg. dashboard.
  • Prediction and Forecast Models (Added Advantage)
    • Build and maintain forecasting models.
  • Model Evaluation (Added Advantage)
    • Evaluate model accuracy by using suitable metrics.
  • Data Analysis
    • Collects, cleans, and analyzes data to identify trends, patterns, and insights to improve procurement strategies.
  • Process Improvement (Added Advantage)
    • Identifies process gaps, proposing solutions and build the solution to improve work efficiency.
  • Any Other Tasks as Assigned
Requirements

  • Able to think from business perspective first.
  • A background in analytics, business, economics, engineering, mathematics, or related fields.
  • 1–2 years of experience in retail, supply chain, FMCG, or procurement is preferred.
  • Proficiency in data analysis tools (Excel, SQL, BI tools, Python (pandas, sklearn)) and structured thinking.
  • Strong ability to communicate analytical findings to stakeholders.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

MR D.I.Y. is a home-grown enterprise with over 1,300 stores across Malaysia and Brunei. The home improvement retailer has dedicated to make a positive difference in the lives of its valued customers by offering convenience at all its stores nationwide and online at mrdiy.com.my

All MR. D.I.Y. stores are managed directly and the retailer often works in collaboration with owners of shopfront properties or owners of malls. MR. D.I.Y. stores offer a wide selection of SKUs across five major categories, namely hardware; household and furnishing; electrical; stationery and sports equipment products; and others (comprising toys, car accessories, jewellery, cosmetics, food and beverage items, as well as health and personal care).

The Company strives to put its customers first by operating an innovative business that is flexible when it comes to providing a wide variety of products, good quality and value-for-money, holding true to the Company’s motto of “Always Low Prices”.

raw_html:
Job Summary:

We are looking for a Junior Application Maintenance / Production Support Engineer to support and maintain enterprise applications under the CEW & PDR platforms. The candidate will be responsible for Level 3 (L3) production support, issue investigation, application maintenance, and ensuring system stability in a banking or enterprise environment.


The ideal candidate should have strong Java Spring Boot development knowledge, Linux administration skills, and experience working with Oracle databases.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide L3 application maintenance and production support for CEW & PDR applications.
  • Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve application and system-related incidents within SLA timelines.
  • Monitor application health, system performance, and batch processing activities.
  • Support deployment, configuration, and maintenance activities across multiple environments.
  • Work closely with development, infrastructure, and business teams to resolve technical issues.
  • Perform root cause analysis (RCA) and implement preventive measures for recurring incidents.
  • Develop and maintain technical documentation, support procedures, and operational reports.
  • Assist in application enhancements, bug fixes, and minor development activities.
  • Ensure system availability, security compliance, and operational stability.
  • Participate in release activities, production validation, and post-deployment support.


Required Skills:

  • 3–5 years of experience in application support, maintenance, or backend development roles.
  • Strong hands-on experience in Java Spring Boot development.
  • Good knowledge of Unix/Linux environments, preferably RHEL or equivalent Linux distributions such as SUSE Linux.
  • Experience working with Oracle Database 12c / 19c.
  • Familiarity with SQL and database troubleshooting activities.
  • Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills.
  • Good communication and coordination skills in cross-functional team environments.


Nice-to-Have Skills:

  • Experience supporting in-house solutions such as Client Entitlement Workflow (CEW) and Payment Data Repository (PDR).
  • Knowledge of Oracle WebLogic and JBoss application servers.
  • Experience with Apache Web Server configuration and support.
  • Familiarity with Shell Scripting and automation tasks.
  • Experience with PL/SQL including procedures, views, and indexes.
  • Exposure to workflow systems and enterprise process management platforms.
  • Understanding of banking application support and production incident management processes.


**Open to Malaysians and expatriates who do not require a visa to work in Malaysia.

raw_html:
This is where your work makes a difference.

At Baxter, we believe every person—regardless of who they are or where they are from—deserves a chance to live a healthy life. It was our founding belief in 1931 and continues to be our guiding principle. We are redefining healthcare delivery to make a greater impact today, tomorrow, and beyond.

Our Baxter colleagues are united by our Mission to Save and Sustain Lives. Together, our community is driven by a culture of courage, trust, and collaboration. Every individual is empowered to take ownership and make a meaningful impact. We strive for efficient and effective operations, and we hold each other accountable for delivering exceptional results.

Here, you will find more than just a job—you will find purpose and pride. 


Role Summary
  • As a Vendor Master Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in ensuring the accuracy and integrity of Baxter’s global vendor records. This position offers the opportunity to work at the center of our Procure‑to‑Pay process, supporting compliance, efficiency, and continuous improvement across the business. You’ll collaborate with Procurement, IT, Finance, and global teams to implement Baxter’s vendor master data strategy, while gaining valuable exposure to international tax reporting and governance practices.



What You’ll Do
  • Take ownership of Baxter’s global vendor master data environment, ensuring vendor creation, changes, and deactivations are accurate and compliant.
  • Contribute to the rollout of Baxter’s vendor master data strategy, standards, and improvement initiatives.
  • Strengthen internal controls and support SOX compliance, safeguarding the integrity of vendor data.
  • Partner with global teams as countries and sites transition into Baxter Business Services.
  • Review and refine vendor master policies to reflect evolving market, company, and industry practices.
  • Identify opportunities for continuous improvement and present solutions that enhance efficiency and accuracy.

What You’ll Bring
  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline.
  • Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in vendor master data, Procure‑to‑Pay processes, or related fields.
  • Strong understanding of P2P processes, systems, and policies.
  • Expertise in master data management best practices, including governance, policies, and technologies.
  • Knowledge of global vendor tax reporting requirements.
  • Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with cross‑functional teams.
  • Excellent communication skills to engage stakeholders and promote process improvements.
  • Experience in change management, with the ability to guide and train internal customers.
  • Strong organizational skills to manage priorities and deliver results.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to assess risk, apply quality standards, and provide root‑cause analysis.
  • Solid knowledge of financial systems and business acumen.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Baxter is an equal opportunity employer. Baxter evaluates qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, disability/handicap status or any other legally protected characteristic.

Reasonable Accommodations

Baxter is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities globally. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application or interview process, please click on the link here and let us know the nature of your request along with your contact information.

Recruitment Fraud Notice

Baxter has discovered incidents of employment scams, where fraudulent parties pose as Baxter employees, recruiters, or other agents, and engage with online job seekers in an attempt to steal personal and/or financial information. To learn how you can protect yourself, review our Recruitment Fraud Notice.
