Company Description MSP Hitect is a professional services and technology consultancy that supports organizations in planning, executing, and governing complex projects and programs. The company focuses on delivering structured project management practices and PMO (Project Management Office) services to help clients improve efficiency, transparency, and business outcomes. MSP Hitect partners with clients across various industries, providing analytical, management, and technical expertise. Team members collaborate closely with client stakeholders, contributing to strategic initiatives while gaining exposure to diverse projects and professional growth opportunities.
Role Description The PMO Analyst – Mandarin is a contract, on-site position based in Shah Alam. This role supports the Project Management Office by tracking project performance, preparing reports, and ensuring adherence to governance standards and methodologies. The PMO Analyst will consolidate project data, maintain project and portfolio documentation, and assist in resource and timeline coordination across multiple initiatives. The role involves frequent communication with project managers and stakeholders, including Mandarin-speaking counterparts, to clarify requirements, resolve issues, and support decision-making. The PMO Analyst will also help identify risks and dependencies, propose improvements to PMO processes, and provide administrative and analytical support to program and project teams.
Qualifications
  • Candidates should possess strong Analytical Skills and the ability to interpret data, identify trends, and provide actionable insights.
  • Candidates should possess Program Management and Project Management capabilities to support planning, monitoring, and coordination of multiple initiatives.
  • Candidates should possess Project Portfolio Management skills to assist in prioritizing, tracking, and reporting across a portfolio of projects.
  • Candidates should possess clear and professional Communication skills, including proficiency in Mandarin and English for stakeholder interaction and documentation.
  • Candidates should possess proficiency in common PM tools (e.g., MS Excel, PowerPoint, project tracking systems) and a solid understanding of PMO governance and methodologies.
  • Candidates should possess a relevant academic background, such as a degree or diploma in Business, Management, IT, Engineering, or a related field, and prior experience in a PMO or project support role.
  • Candidates should possess strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work on-site in Shah Alam within a fast-paced, client-focused environment.

raw_html:
About The Role:

We’re looking for a ServiceNow Developer who will help take ownership & develop our ‘Connect’ (ServiceNow) platform in Demant – and help us push the boundaries of it. You’ll play a key role in designing, developing, and optimizing solutions across the platform and business, ensure they are scalable, secure & follow best practice, and are aligned with business needs. It’s a hands-on technical role with real influence. You’ll be part of our ‘ITSM’ team working with skilled colleagues across both development and operations of the platform.


Responsibilities

  • Develop & Operate the ServiceNow platform (“Connect”) across Demant
  • Design best-practice solution & standards to ensure a stable, scalable & reliable platform
  • Own your solutions end‑to‑end – from idea & design to implementation & operations
  • Continuous development & improvement of the platform and modules – today we have ITSM, CMDB, ITOM, Workflow Data Fabric, and HRSD modules
  • Help shape our ServiceNow roadmap and best practices through close sparring with leadership, stakeholders and team
  • Scale ‘IT solutions’ to Enterprise level – with focus on driving efficiency for our users


Requirements

  • Solid experience as ServiceNow developer understanding the platform setup & capabilities
  • Strong understanding and experience within CMDB (CSDM), ITOM ‘discovery & service mapping’, and data modelling is key for the perfect candidate
  • Comfortable with scripting (JavaScript/Glide) and setup integrations after best-practice (REST, SOAP, MID Server, Workflow Data Fabric).
  • The ability to take ownership, support or make decisions on your own, and drive solutions forward
  • Ability to speak up on solutions or decisions you don’t agree to and challenge status quo
  • A collaborative mindset – you enjoy working with others, not just coding in a corner


Join an organisation based on Trust, Openness, and Innovation:

In Demant you will be part of an innovative and challenging environment based on trust and openness, where you will experience a strong will to achieve great results with some ambitious and talented colleagues. You will be part of an environment where flexibility, knowledge sharing, and professional respect make it challenging, meaningful, and fun to go to work every day.

We are a great mix of colleagues who enjoy an open and friendly tone as well as a culture of inclusiveness and mutual support, echoing the values found throughout our organization.


We Offer:

  • A job in a growing, international company with good career opportunities
  • State of the art technology and work in an exciting industry
  • Good work life balance with flexible working conditions
  • A culture of teamwork, based on knowledge sharing, professional respect, kindness
  • We believe in, and encourage, open and honest communication, and we hope to put a smile on your face every workday
  • A full-time independent and responsible position
  • Training, conferences, and the opportunity for self-development
  • We offer the opportunity to travel for work globally


About Demant:

Demant is a world-leading hearing healthcare group built on a heritage of care, health and innovation since 1904. The Group offers innovative technologies, solutions and expertise to help people hear better. In every aspect, from hearing care and hearing aids to diagnostic equipment and services, Demant is active and engaged. Headquartered in Denmark, the Group employs more than 26,000 people globally and is present with solutions in 130 countries creating life-changing differences through hearing health. William Demant Foundation holds the majority of shares in Demant A/S, which is listed on Nasdaq Copenhagen and among the 25 most traded stocks.

At Demant, we value diversity, equity, and inclusion (DE&I) and we are committed to creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of ethnic background, personality, age, gender, and education. Diversity is part of our cultural DNA. Women in STEM areas are particularly encouraged to apply, as Demant supports United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 5 on gender equality.

raw_html:
We are seeking a skilled and motivated ServiceNow Developer to join our IT team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, configuring, and customizing solutions within the ServiceNow platform to support business and IT needs. You will work with cross-functional teams to implement and optimize workflows, integrations, and user experiences across various ServiceNow modules.

Key Responsibilities:


  • Design and develop customized solutions within the ServiceNow platform, including custom applications, workflows, and UI components.
  • Work with stakeholders to gather and analyze requirements and translate them into technical specifications.
  • Develop and maintain modules including (but not limited to) ITSM, ITOM, CMDB, HRSD, SecOps, or GRC, depending on project needs.
  • Integrate ServiceNow with other enterprise systems using REST/SOAP APIs, IntegrationHub, or custom scripts.
  • Maintain and optimize platform performance, reliability, and usability.
  • Create and maintain technical documentation, including design specs, user guides, and release notes.
  • Ensure code quality through testing, peer reviews, and adherence to best practices.
  • Support day-to-day platform operations, including incident resolution, enhancements, and platform upgrades.
  • Collaborate with ServiceNow administrators, architects, and other developers to implement scalable solutions.


    • Required Skills and Qualifications:


  • 2–5+ years of hands-on development experience with ServiceNow.
  • Strong understanding of ServiceNow architecture, data model, and platform capabilities.
  • Proficient in JavaScript, Glide scripting, UI Policies, Business Rules, Client Scripts, and Flow Designer.
  • Experience with ServiceNow Studio, Update Sets, and Application Scoping.
  • Familiar with REST/SOAP integrations, MID Server, and IntegrationHub.
  • Knowledge of ITIL processes and best practices.
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams.


    • Preferred Qualifications:


  • ServiceNow Certified System Administrator (CSA) – required/preferred.
  • One or more of the following certifications:
  • Certified Application Developer (CAD)
  • Certified Implementation Specialist (CIS) – [ITSM / ITOM / HR etc.]
  • Experience with Agile methodologies and tools such as Jira or ServiceNow Agile.

    • Exposure to Scoped Applications and Custom App Development

raw_html:
Role Description

  • Develop and maintain high-quality mobile applications for iOS and Android platforms.
  • Architect and implement robust backend services and web applications using Spring Boot, PHP, and Laravel.
  • Design and build responsive, user-centric front-end interfaces utilizing Vue.js.
  • Collaborate with UX/UI designers, product managers, and cross-functional teams to deliver end-to-end features and product enhancements.
  • Write clean, modular, and well-documented code across both mobile and web environments.
  • Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize applications for peak performance and security.
  • Integrate complex third-party APIs and libraries into both mobile and web ecosystems.
  • Stay ahead of emerging trends and technologies in both mobile and web development landscapes.
  • Perform thorough code reviews, providing technical mentorship and constructive feedback.
  • Operate within Agile methodologies, actively contributing to sprint planning, daily stand-ups, and retrospectives.

 

Qualifications

  • Welcome Talent at Any Career Stage: We encourage both fresh graduates with a strong portfolio and experienced developers to apply and grow with us. 
  • Mobile Proficiency: Deep expertise in Android Studio (Java) and Swift.
  • Web Proficiency: Strong command of Vue.js for frontend, and Spring Boot, Java, PHP, and Laravel for robust backend development.
  • Solid understanding of mobile architecture, web best practices, and system integration.
  • Hands-on experience with performance optimization, memory management, and debugging.
  • Proficiency in version control systems, specifically Git.
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with the ability to work in a collaborative team environment.
  • Excellent communication skills; bilingual proficiency in Mandarin and English is highly desired.


Location: Plaza Arkadia, Kuala Lumpur

Salary: RM 3,500 – RM 7,500 per month

raw_html:
As the largest pureplay adhesives company in the world, H.B. Fuller’s (NYSE: FUL) innovative, functional coatings, adhesives and sealants enhance the quality, safety and performance of products people use every day. Founded in 1887, with 2024 revenue of $3.6 billion, our mission to Connect What Matters is brought to life by more than 7,500 global team members who collaborate with customers across more than 30 market segments in over 140 countries to develop highly specified solutions that enable customers to bring world-changing innovations to their end markets. Learn more at www.hbfuller.com.

Position Overview

The Strategic Sourcing Analyst supports H.B. Fuller’s packaging sourcing efforts by combining data-driven analysis with hands-on supplier engagement. This role focuses on direct sourcing activities, competitive bidding, and spend optimization across industrial packaging materials. Working closely with sourcing managers and cross-functional partners, the analyst contributes to supply assurance, cost management, and continuous improvement.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Source and manage industrial packaging categories.
  • Conduct RFQs, competitive bids, and supplier evaluations to support category strategies
  • Analyze spend, pricing, and volume data to identify cost-saving and compliance opportunities
  • Maintain accurate pricing, contracts, and supplier information within ERP systems
  • Collaborate with suppliers and internal stakeholders to resolve pricing and invoice discrepancies
  • Track category volumes and performance against contracts, rebates, and commitments
  • Support system and process improvements related to sourcing and purchasing workflows
  • Rotate spend responsibility across categories in alignment with team priorities


Minimum Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in supply chain, business, engineering, or related field, or equivalent experience
  • 1–2 years of experience in sourcing, procurement, supply chain, or operations
  • Experience working with suppliers, pricing, quotes, or bids
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and ERP or purchasing systems
  • Authorization to work in the United States without sponsorship


Preferred Requirements

  • Experience sourcing packaging or industrial materials
  • Exposure to RFQ/RFP processes and bid analysis
  • Familiarity with spend analysis, forecasting, or contract compliance


H.B. Fuller is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer and proud to have created a collaborative culture where employees around the world are seen, heard, and respected. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, or marital status or status as a protected veteran, or any other legally protected classification.

H.B. Fuller does not accept unsolicited resumes from recruiters, employment agencies, or staffing firms. To conduct business with H.B. Fuller, a written service agreement must be executed by Human Resources prior to submitting any information relating to a potential candidate. Without a signed service agreement, H.B. Fuller shall not be obligated for payment of any fee or compensation.

raw_html:
Date: 24 Jun 2026

Service Line / Portfolios: Engineering, AI & Data

Location:

Kuala Lumpur, MY

Are you ready to unleash your potential?

At Deloitte, our purpose is to make an impact that matters for our clients, our people, and the communities we serve.

We believe we have a responsibility to be a force for good, and WorldImpact is our portfolio of initiatives focused on making a tangible impact on society’s biggest challenges and creating a better future. We strive to advise clients on how to deliver purpose-led growth and embed more equitable, inclusive as well as sustainable business practices.

Hence, we seek talented individuals driven to excel and innovate, working together to achieve our shared goals.

We are committed to creating positive work experiences that foster a culture of respect and inclusion, where diverse perspectives are celebrated, and everyone is recognised for their contributions.

Ready to unleash your potential with us? Join the winning team now!

Work you’ll do:

  • You will develop cross-platform mobile applications using React Native, targeting iOS, Android, and Huawei devices.
  • You will be involved in database design and development, including integration with mobile applications via APIs and backend services.
  • You will conduct primary and secondary research, perform analyses and technical modelling that inform the design and implementation of technology-enabled mobile solutions addressing our clients’ complex business challenges.
  • You will apply your training in software development, leveraging React Native expertise, analytical thinking, and strong communication skills to support project teams in delivering robust digital architectures and mobile work products aligned with client objectives.
  • You will assist project teams in developing presentations and demos, and help coordinate communication with clients throughout the project lifecycle.
  • You will support the delivery of business-driven, technology-enabled mobile solutions that help clients solve critical issues and capitalize on opportunities within their industries.
  • You will collaborate with a diverse, multidisciplinary project team to tackle technical problems, enhance mobile application performance, and deliver tangible value to our clients.
  • You will uphold the firm’s standards, values, and collaborative culture in your interactions with team members and client stakeholders.
  • You will contribute to business development activities, including the preparation of technical proposals, project presentations, and internal/external publications.

Your role as a leader:

At Deloitte, we believe in the importance of empowering our people to be leaders at all levels. We connect our purpose and shared values to identify issues as well as to make an impact that matters to our clients, people and communities. Additionally, experience analysts/consultants across our Firm are expected to:

  • Demonstrate a strong commitment to personal learning and development.
  • Understand how our daily work contributes to the priorities of the team and business.
  • Understand the set expectations and demonstrate accountability in keeping personal performance on track.
  • Actively focus on developing effective communications and relationship-building skills with stakeholders, clients and team.
  • Demonstrate an appreciation for working with others.
  • Understand what is fundamental to Deloitte’s success as a business.
  • Demonstrate integrity and an awareness of strengths, differences, and personal impact.
  • Develop their understanding of Deloitte and offer a fresh perspective.

Requirements:

  • You will be responsible for management and delivery of a system(s) within a platform leveraging agile practices, by leveraging existing experience of working in an agile environment.
  • The right person will be a detailed and hands-on developer with at least 5 – 8 years of relevant experience
  • Good programming languages: JavaScript/TypeScript
  • Strong technical skills in;
    • Mobile Development – React Native
  • Experience in delivering solutions on iOS/Android
  • Technical proficiency including systems development experience, strong troubleshooting and problem- solving skills, and issue management in a multi-platform environment.
  • Experience with native development or bridging in Swift, Obj-C, Kotlin and Java is an advantage.
  • Demonstrates skills in communication (oral, written, presentation), analysis, problem solving and short term and long-term planning
  • Demonstrated portfolio of work showcasing technical competence
  • An appreciation of the consulting lifestyle and ability to travel (both locally and abroad) is a pre-requisite to fit to our short-term and long-term project assignment.

Due to volume of applications, we regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Please note that Deloitte will never reach out to you directly via messaging platforms to offer you employment opportunities or request for money or your personal information. Kindly apply for roles that you are interested in via this official Deloitte website.

Requisition ID: 114023

In Malaysia, the services are provided by Deloitte and other related entities in Malaysia ("Deloitte in Malaysia"), which are affiliates of Deloitte Southeast Asia Ltd. Deloitte Southeast Asia Ltd is a member firm of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited. Deloitte in Malaysia, which is within the Deloitte Network, is the entity that is providing this Website.

Company: 2201 United Overseas Bank (Malaysia) BhdAbout UOBUnited Overseas Bank Limited (UOB) is a leading bank in Asia with a global network of more than 500 branches and offices in 19 countries and territories in Asia Pacific, Europe and North America. In Asia, we operate through our head office in Singapore and banking subsidiaries in China, Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand, as well as branches and offices. Our history spans more than 80 years. Over this time, we have been guided by our values – Honorable, Enterprising, United and Committed. This means we always strive to do what is right, build for the future, work as one team and pursue long-term success. It is how we work, consistently, be it towards the company, our colleagues or our customers.Job DescriptionSales Performance ManagementTo support Sales Management Reporting: Sales Performance Measurement Matrix; Revenue & Product Achievement Analysis / Penetration Analysis Reporting; for the purpose of assessing each team/branch or region’s strengths and weaknesses in order to develop appropriate business and strategic plan for each region with different nature.To monitor the achievement of non-performing staff, and report to management on their progress monthly.To provide sales activities tracking tools and monitor to ensure sales activities are implemented as planned within budget, and expected leads / sales closure are met.To support the design & implementation of tactical campaigns for respective channels or products to boost sales & assist the management in ensuring the budget are met.To support the Development and continuously improve the online system for performance management, sales analysis & auto-computation of commission.Sales KPI / PEAK ManagementTo support the design & implementation of the KPIs for ACGTo support & provide monthly update on the Service KPIs.Sales Incentive, Tactical Compensation & Planning SupportTo support the design & implementation the Sales Incentive Scheme, for the purpose of motivating the sales & service team & assist the management in ensuring the budget are met.Sales ComplianceTo support & monitor and reinforce sales compliance guidelines to ensure staff awareness e.g. mis-selling, conflict of interest, etc.Additional RequirementsDevelop, Engage, Execute, StrategiseBe a Part of the UOB FamilyUOB is an equal opportunity employer. United Overseas Bank Limited (UOB) is a leading bank in Asia with a global network of more than 500 branches and offices in 19 countries and territories in Asia Pacific, Europe and North America. In Asia, we operate through our head office in Singapore and banking subsidiaries in China, Indonesia, Malaysia and Thailand, as well as branches and offices. Our history spans more than 80 years. Over this time, we have been guided by our values – Honorable, Enterprising, United and Committed. This means we always strive to do what is right, build for the future, work as one team and pursue long-term success. It is how we work, consistently, be it towards the company, our colleagues or our customers.

Job Description

Sales Performance Management

  • To support Sales Management Reporting: Sales Performance Measurement Matrix; Revenue & Product Achievement Analysis / Penetration Analysis Reporting; for the purpose of assessing each team/branch or region’s strengths and weaknesses in order to develop appropriate business and strategic plan for each region with different nature.
  • To monitor the achievement of non-performing staff, and report to management on their progress monthly.
  • To provide sales activities tracking tools and monitor to ensure sales activities are implemented as planned within budget, and expected leads / sales closure are met.
  • To support the design & implementation of tactical campaigns for respective channels or products to boost sales & assist the management in ensuring the budget are met.
  • To support the Development and continuously improve the online system for performance management, sales analysis & auto-computation of commission.

Sales KPI / PEAK Management

  • To support the design & implementation of the KPIs for ACG
  • To support & provide monthly update on the Service KPIs.

Sales Incentive, Tactical Compensation & Planning Support

  • To support the design & implementation the Sales Incentive Scheme, for the purpose of motivating the sales & service team & assist the management in ensuring the budget are met.

Sales Compliance

  • To support & monitor and reinforce sales compliance guidelines to ensure staff awareness e.g. mis-selling, conflict of interest, etc.

Additional Requirements

Develop, Engage, Execute, Strategise

Be a Part of the UOB Family

UOB is an equal opportunity employer. UOB does not discriminate on the basis of a candidate's age, race, gender, color, religion, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, or other non-merit factors. All employment decisions at UOB are based on business needs, job requirements and qualifications. If you require any assistance or accommodations to be made for the recruitment process, please inform us when you submit your online application.

Apply now and make a Difference

raw_html:
Job Description

Help reduce risk and improve outcomes by resolving complex initial margin disputes with speed and precision. You'll work on high-impact investigations that sit at the intersection of markets, data, operations, and documentation. Join a collaborative team where your judgment and analysis directly influence timely resolution for key stakeholders. You'll build deep expertise through hands-on casework and continual learning. Bring your problem-solving skills and make a measurable difference.

As a Margin and Collateral Associate within the Collateral and Margin function, you will investigate and resolve initial margin disputes across end-to-end collateral workflows. You will use quantitative analysis (including sensitivity-based approaches) to explain margin changes with clear, data-backed narratives. You will partner with trading, risk, credit, legal, operations, and technology teams to identify root causes and agree remediation. You will help ensure dispute outcomes align with governing documentation and agreed contractual terms. You will contribute to continuous improvement by recognizing recurring patterns and strengthening controls and processes.

Job Responsibilities

  • Investigate initial margin disputes end-to-end, from issue identification through resolution and closure
  • Analyze margin movements using sensitivity-based and data-driven techniques to explain drivers clearly
  • Reconcile differences across trades, market data, risk factors, and configuration or model assumptions
  • Document findings, decisions, and evidence in a clear, audit-ready manner
  • Coordinate with counterparties and internal partners to align on root cause and remediation actions
  • Apply documentation concepts (e.g., credit support and initial margin terms) when evaluating dispute outcomes
  • Escalate appropriately when numbers do not reconcile, using facts to drive timely decisions
  • Identify recurring break patterns and recommend process or control improvements to prevent reoccurrence
  • Track dispute status, timelines, and deliverables to ensure commitments are met
  • Support operational excellence through continuous improvement and knowledge sharing

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills

  • Minimum 5years of experience in collateral management, margin operations, dispute investigation, or reconciliation roles supporting derivatives
  • Ability to perform structured root-cause analysis across complex operational and data issues
  • Working knowledge of initial margin concepts and key drivers of margin movement
  • Demonstrated capability to produce clear, evidence-based written summaries for senior stakeholders
  • Experience partnering cross-functionally (e.g., trading, risk, credit, legal, operations, technology) to drive issue resolution
  • Proficiency in Excel for analysis and reconciliations
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to prioritize multiple time-sensitive investigations

Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, And Skills

  • Experience investigating initial margin disputes using sensitivity-based methodology (e.g., ISDA Standard Initial Margin Model)
  • Familiarity with an industry dispute workflow platform used for margin calls and case management
  • Ability to query datasets using SQL and/or perform analysis in Python
  • Exposure to derivatives risk factors, sensitivities, and market data sourcing considerations
  • Familiarity with credit support and initial margin documentation concepts and dispute timelines
  • Experience operating in a controlled, regulated environment with strong process discipline
  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience

ABOUT US

J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first-class business in a first-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

About The Team

J.P. Morgan's Commercial & Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial & Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

raw_html:
Job Summary:

Although no two days at Accenture are the same, as an Intelligence & Insights (I&I) Consultant in our Talent & Organization (T&O) practice, a typical day might include:

Comfortable with developing and deploying data-driven solutions to People transformation efforts for Accenture’s Global and Local clients. Strong problem-solving, decision-making, and stakeholder interaction is critical for the role.

Key Responsibilities:


  • Engage with clients to understand their challenges and recommend building, orchestrating, and running complex AI workflows and agents using large language models (LLMs)
  • Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets to develop business solutions by determining the best I&I solutions.
  • Build strong relationships with clients and foster business growth with clear communication and stakeholder engagement.
  • Define data-backed people strategies to support business transformational programs
  • Co-create solutions and assist clients in developing action plans
  • Contribute to T&O practice-building efforts


    • Key Qualifications:


  • Self-learners with hands-on expertise in Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), Agentic AI, RAG framework, LangChain/LangGraph, vector DB, FAISS and delivering real world AI impact at scale.
  • 4 to 6 years of experience in developing and deploying applications on cloud platforms using python along with few years of experience in delivering complex AI workflows and agents using LLMs
  • Working knowledge of MLOps, including building and managing end-to-end ML pipelines, Model deployment to production environments, safety guardrails, monitoring and maintaining deployed models in Azure and AWS
  • Working knowledge on Natural Language Processing (NLP) including TensorFlow, BERT, Transformer architecture, Hugging face models.
  • Good to have domain knowledge in people analytics and people management-related programs (workforce transformation, workforce M&A strategy, culture transformations, I&D strategy etc.)
  • Data fluency, data analysis and storytelling, and working knowledge of statistical methodologies.
  • Fluency in English is required, additional language capabilities are highly advantageous
  • Ability to perform in a non-structured and dynamic environment


    • Additional Information:


  • Natural leader; easily establishes relationships with clients and colleagues.
  • Team Player; enjoys the intellectual stimulation of collaborating with colleagues around the world.
  • Determined; appreciates a challenge and overcoming obstacles to achieve results.
  • Digitally savvy; continuous learner.
  • A transparent career path designed for rapid career progression.
  • A supportive culture that is serious about training, coaching and continuous learning.
  • A global network where only state-of-the-art tools and technologies will do.
  • A bighearted environment with opportunities to give back to our local communities.

raw_html:
Date: 25 Jun 2026

Service Line / Portfolios: Information Technology

Location:

Kuala Lumpur, MY

Are you ready to unleash your potential?

At Deloitte, our purpose is to make an impact that matters for our clients, our people, and the communities we serve.

We believe we have a responsibility to be a force for good, and WorldImpact is our portfolio of initiatives focused on making a tangible impact on society’s biggest challenges and creating a better future. We strive to advise clients on how to deliver purpose-led growth and embed more equitable, inclusive as well as sustainable business practices.

Hence, we seek talented individuals driven to excel and innovate, working together to achieve our shared goals.

We are committed to creating positive work experiences that foster a culture of respect and inclusion, where diverse perspectives are celebrated, and everyone is recognised for their contributions.

Ready to unleash your potential with us? Join the winning team now!

Work you’ll do

As the Penetration Testing Analyst, you will be responsible for providing penetration testing services through a combination of technology and manual ingenuity as part of the Global cyber services organization for member firms.

Responsibilities Of This Role Include

  • Assisting in technical scoping of security testing activities
  • Executing security testing
  • Web Application Penetration Testing
  • Web Services / Application Programming Interface (API) Penetration Testing
  • Network Penetration Testing
  • Mobile Application Penetration Testing
  • Thick Client Penetration Testing
  • Conducting focused research when not deployed on an active project
  • Provide consultative guidance to customers on findings identified in a clear and actionable fashion both in writing and verbally
  • Enhancing and updating testing methodologies, processes and standards documentation
  • Maintaining proficiency of knowledge through ongoing training paths
  • Proficient at analyzing and understanding complex architecture designs
  • Ability to effectively communicate what services and capabilities our group can facilitate to our clients and responsibilities here.

Your role as a leader

At Deloitte, We Believe In The Importance Of Empowering Our People To Be Leaders At All Levels. We Connect Our Purpose And Shared Values To Identify Issues As Well As To Make An Impact That Matters To Our Clients, People And The Communities. Additionally, Analysts Across Our Firm Are Expected To

  • Demonstrate a strong commitment to personal learning and development.
  • Understand how our daily work contributes to the priorities of the team and business.
  • Understand the set expectations and demonstrate accountability in keeping personal performance on track.
  • Actively focus on developing effective communications and relationship-building skills with stakeholders, clients and team.
  • Demonstrate an appreciation for working with others.
  • Understand what is fundamental to Deloitte’s success as a business.
  • Demonstrate integrity and an awareness of strengths, differences, and personal impact.
  • Develop their understanding of Deloitte and offer a fresh perspective.

Requirements

  • Demonstrated min 3 years' experience of operating in a penetration testing role.
  • Experience using common testing tools like Burp, OWASP ZAP, Metasploit, Postman, Swagger, NMAP, Qualys, SQLMap, others
  • Experienced with Kali Linux or other dedicated Penetration Testing OS Platform
  • Advance mobile penetration testing, Application Penetration Testing and architectural security principles
  • Familiarity with software security weakness and vulnerabilities
  • Working knowledge of one scripting language and familiarity with at least one software programming language and framework
  • Demonstrated experience working with diverse stakeholders, preferably on a global multi-national basis
  • Ability to manage concurrent initiatives and use effective judgment in prioritization and time management
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills

Preferred

  • Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH) Certification
  • Offensive Certified Security Professional (OSCP) Certification
  • Any GIAC Certification (GSEC, GWAB, GPEN, GMOB, GCPN)
  • OWASP Application Security Top 10
  • OWASP API Security Top 10
  • OWASP Thick Client Top 10
  • OWASP LLM Top 10
  • MITRE ATT&CK Framework
  • Cloud Service testing
  • Reverse Engineering
  • Static Application Software Testing (SAST)
  • Dynamic Application Testing (DAST)
  • Experience of Agentic development and its application to support penetration testing.

Due to volume of applications, we regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Candidates will only be contacted by authorized Deloitte Recruiters via firm’s business contact number or business email address.

#DRSCGLOBAL

Deloitte Southeast Asia Ltd is a member firm of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited. Deloitte in Malaysia, which is within the Deloitte Network, is the entity that is providing this Website.

Service Line / Portfolios: Assurance

Kuala Lumpur, MY

Title: DRSC Assurance - Software Asset Management (SAM) - Analyst (Fresh Graduates)

Are you ready to unleash your potential?

At Deloitte, our purpose is to make an impact that matters for our clients, our people, and the communities we serve.

We believe we have a responsibility to be a force for good, and WorldImpact is our portfolio of initiatives focused on making a tangible impact on society’s biggest challenges and creating a better future. We strive to advise clients on how to deliver purpose-led growth and embed more equitable, inclusive as well as sustainable business practices.

Hence, we seek talented individuals driven to excel and innovate, working together to achieve our shared goals.

We are committed to creating positive work experiences that foster a culture of respect and inclusion, where diverse perspectives are celebrated, and everyone is recognised for their contributions.

Ready to unleash your potential with us? Join the winning team now!

Work you’ll do

Responsibilities

  • Support software asset management activities throughout the software lifecycle, including software inventory, deployment, usage tracking, and licence compliance.
  • Assist in analysing software usage data to identify optimisation opportunities and support cost-saving initiatives.
  • Work with project teams to prepare reports, documentation, and compliance assessments for clients.
  • Support software licence reviews and help identify potential compliance risks under the guidance of senior team members.
  • Collaborate with clients, software vendors, and internal teams to gather information and support software asset management engagements.
  • Build your knowledge of software licensing models, industry best practices, and emerging technology trends through on-the-job learning and structured development.

Your role as a leader

At Deloitte, We Believe In The Importance Of Empowering Our People To Be Leaders At All Levels. We Connect Our Purpose And Shared Values To Identify Issues As Well As To Make An Impact That Matters To Our Clients, People And The Communities. Additionally, Analysts Across Our Firm Are Expected To

  • Understand how our daily work contributes to the priorities of the team and business.
  • Understand the set expectations and demonstrate accountability in keeping personal performance on track.
  • Actively focus on developing effective communications and relationship-building skills with stakeholders, clients and team.
  • Demonstrate an appreciation for working with others.
  • Understand what is fundamental to Deloitte’s success as a business.
  • Demonstrate integrity and an awareness of strengths, differences, and personal impact.
  • Develop their understanding of Deloitte and offer a fresh perspective.

Requirements

  • A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Information Systems, or a related discipline.
  • 0–2 years of experience in IT, technology consulting, software asset management, IT audit, or a related field.
  • Strong analytical thinking and an interest in technology governance and software compliance.
  • Good written and verbal communication skills.
  • A proactive mindset, eagerness to learn, and the ability to manage multiple priorities.
  • Knowledge of software asset management, ITIL, ISO 19770, or software licensing concepts is an advantage but not essential.

Due to volume of applications, we regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Candidates will only be contacted by authorized Deloitte Recruiters via firm’s business contact number or business email address.
